header.title: Sttok versus Excel
header.description: Most companies start managing their shareholder registry in a spreadsheet. It works until it doesn't: a miscalculated capital increase, an urgent certificate, a due diligence. This is the honest comparison.

rows.excel: Manual formulas; one error propagates silently.
rows.sttok: Percentages and dilutions always calculated on the real registry.
A single source of truth, with history and traceability.

rows.excel: Drafted by hand every time a shareholder asks.
rows.sttok: Generated instantly from each shareholder's file.
Digital convening, proxies, voting and automatic minutes.

rows.excel: Email forwards, no version control or permissions.
rows.sttok: A portal for each shareholder, with role-based permissions.

rows.excel: A shared file, without encryption or access control.
rows.sttok: ISO 27001, GDPR, SSO and data encrypted in Europe.
The whole history and documentation, ready to share.

rows.excel: Free in licenses; expensive in hours and errors.
rows.sttok: From €450 per company per year, with one-week onboarding.

items.q: Can I migrate my current Excel to Sttok?
items.a: Yes. Onboarding includes migrating the registry and its history, and takes one week.

items.q: What happens to my operation history?
items.a: It is loaded into Sttok: capital increases, transfers and other operations are recorded with their documentation.

items.q: What if I only have one small company?
items.a: The Basic plan (€450/year, up to 10 shareholders) covers the registry, incentive plans and the simulator. Excel becomes more expensive as soon as there is one operation.
